title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ketcham recounts past conflicts to Officer Vane, describing an argument three days prior when Mr. Braid crossed the bay to confront Striker in the woods. Paul Roman, noted for his great strength, intervened and forcibly removed Braid from the island, leading Braid to vow to bankrupt his opponents. Ketcham also details a fight involving Jonah Lincoln, who lives at Braid's residence. After Lincoln's sister stripped naked in front of Roman to protest her brother's control, Lincoln slapped Roman, prompting Roman to throw Lincoln into the bay.

Following these revelations, Vane, Professor Yadley, and an agitated Dr. Temple return to the mainland. While Ellery and Professor Yadley spend a quiet evening discussing university memories, Dr. Temple departs his home in the middle of the night. He moves stealthily through the woods of Bradwood toward the Lein residence. 

Crouching near a window at the Lein house, Dr. Temple eavesdrops on a tense conversation between Percy and Elizabeth Lein. They discuss something that has been buried and mention a "Bundy Reine incident" in post-war Budapest. Elizabeth expresses fear, but Percy insists they will be safe. After the couple falls silent, Dr. Temple retreats through the woods toward the pier. There, he observes a man and a woman in a rowboat near the shore. The woman pleads to be taken to the island for safety, but the man warns her that the situation is dangerous and that they should remain split up until the crisis passes.
